item.text: I’ll inspect `main.gc`, the GenesisCode documentation, and the relevant evaluator behavior, then make the smallest semantics-preserving change and run the local executable within the ten-step budget.

item.command: /bin/zsh -lc "pwd && printf '%s\\n' '--- workspace ---' && rg --files -g '"'!*.git*'"' . && printf '%s\\n' '--- input ---' && rg --files ../input/repository ../input/tools | head -200"
/bin/zsh -lc "pwd && printf '%s\\n' '--- workspace ---' && rg --files -g '"'!*.git*'"' . && printf '%s\\n' '--- input ---' && rg --files ../input/repository ../input/tools | head -200"

/bin/zsh -lc "printf '%s\\n' '--- main.gc ---' && sed -n '1,240p' main.gc && printf '%s\\n' '--- genesis tool help ---' && ../input/tools/genesis --help 2>&1 | sed -n '1,240p' && printf '%s\\n' '--- likely evaluator docs ---' && rg -n -i \"step|budget|eval|run|entry|main\" ../input/repository/genesisCode.md ../input/repository/reference ../input/repository/learn | head -240"

/bin/zsh -lc "printf '%s\\n' '--- main.gc ---' && sed -n '1,240p' main.gc && printf '%s\\n' '--- genesis tool help ---' && ../input/tools/genesis --help 2>&1 | sed -n '1,240p' && printf '%s\\n' '--- likely evaluator docs ---' && rg -n -i \"step|budget|eval|run|entry|main\" ../input/repository/genesisCode.md ../input/repository/reference ../input/repository/learn | head -240"

Usage: genesis [OPTIONS] <COMMAND>

Commands:
  parse                Parse and canonicalize a CoreForm module without modifying its source file
  fmt                  Canonical formatting for CoreForm (.gc) files
  eval                 Evaluate a CoreForm program/module (pure)
  explain              Explain contract dispatch path for a given message
  run                  Run an effect program with a capability policy and emit a deterministic log
  replay               Replay an effect program deterministically from a log (hard-fails on mismatch)
  debug                Deterministic debug/trace commands for contract dispatch root-cause loops
  test                 Run package obligations (unit tests, determinism, replay checks, etc.) and write evidence into .genesis/store
  pack                 Compute and store a content-addressed package artifact record
  selfhost-artifact    Build a selfhost CoreForm toolchain artifact for bootstrap cutover
  selfhost-dashboard   Emit a selfhost cutover dashboard artifact and markdown mirror
  warm                 Warm startup mode: process versioned newline-delimited JSON frames in one process
  mcp                  Serve the pinned Model Context Protocol interface over stdio
  cli-schema           Emit machine-readable CLI command/option schema for agent planning
  agent-index          Emit AI-facing planning index or one exact symbol/diagnostic record
  agent-plan           Plan a deterministic workflow DAG from structured agent intent with policy prechecks
  bench                Predeclare, execute, validate, replay, bundle, and publish GenesisBench runs
  keygen               Generate a new Ed25519 signing key
  sign                 Sign a package acceptance artifact and record the signature in the evidence store
  transparency-verify  Verify the local transparency log chain (if present)
  typecheck            Run the type/effect checker for a package
  optimize             Optimize a CoreForm module/program (pure subset only)
  apply-patch          Validate and apply a semantic patch, then re-run package obligations
  session              Isolated, content-addressed agent transaction sessions
  semantic-edit        Semantic edit tooling for agentic patch planning [aliases: semedit]
  verify               Verify package hashes and evidence store integrity
  store                Content-addressed store operations (effectful; policy-gated)
  refs                 Manage refs (branches/tags) as effectful operations (policy-gated)
  commit               Create and inspect GenesisGraph commit artifacts
  pkg                  GenesisPkg/GCPM operations (snapshot + bundle export/import) [aliases: gcpm]
  policy               Local policy alias/default management
  sync                 Sync artifacts and refs with a remote registry (effectful; policy-gated)
  registry             First-party Genesis registry server operations
  gc                   Garbage-collect the local artifact store using reachability closure from refs + locks + pins
  vcs                  GenesisGraph commit DAG operations
  help                 Print this message or the help of the given subcommand(s)

Options:
      --json
          Emit machine-readable JSON on stdout

      --step-limit <N>
          Kernel evaluation step limit (default is toolchain-defined)

      --no-step-limit
          Disable the kernel evaluation step limit

      --max-alloc-units <N>
          Maximum cumulative logical allocation units during evaluation

      --max-live-units <N>
          Maximum logical units reachable from evaluator roots at a safe point

      --max-pair-cells <N>
          Maximum total number of `pair/cons` cells allocated during evaluation

      --max-vec-len <N>
          Maximum observed vector length (vector literals and `vec/push`)

      --max-map-len <N>
          Maximum observed map length (map literals, `map/put`, `map/merge`)

      --max-bytes-len <N>
          Maximum observed bytes length (bytes literals and `bytes/concat`)

      --max-string-len <N>
          Maximum observed string length in UTF-8 bytes (string literals and `str/concat`)

      --selfhost-artifact <FILE>
          Path to selfhost toolchain artifact used when `--engine selfhost` is selected. Defaults to `./.genesis/selfhost/toolchain.gc` when bootstrap mode allows artifacts

      --selfhost-bootstrap <SELFHOST_BOOTSTRAP>
          Selfhost bootstrap mode. Accepted value: artifact-only.
          
          [default: artifact-only]
          [possible values: artifact-only]

      --selfhost-only
          Enforce selfhost-only execution for frontend paths.
          
          In this mode, commands that accept `--engine` must use `--engine selfhost`, and selfhost bootstrap mode must be `artifact-only` (no embedded fallback). This can also be enabled via `GENESIS_SELFHOST_ONLY=1`.

      --coreform-frontend <COREFORM_FRONTEND>
          CoreForm frontend. Accepted value: selfhost.

  -h, --help
          Print help (see a summary with '-h')

  -V, --version
          Print version

../input/repository/genesisCode.md:13:GenesisCode v0.2 is an AI-native programming system built around a sharply minimized **pure kernel** and an aggressively modern **verification-and-provenance envelope** that governs how programs evolve—whether written by humans or proposed by large language models (LLMs). The kernel is a small functional calculus (**Gλ**) with immutable data, lexical closures, and a single universal abstraction—**contracts**—that implement message-based behavior and delegation through an explicit, inspectable protocol. Unlike “minimalist manifesto languages,” GenesisCode v0.2 makes its core claims mechanically enforceable: it standardizes **unforgeable seals** to harden protocol invariants; it pushes all nondeterminism (I/O, time, randomness, network, LLM calls) behind a capability boundary with **deterministic effect logs** and replay semantics; and it introduces an **Obligation Engine** that requires packages and extensions to carry evidence artifacts (tests, property checks, proofs, resource budgets, determinism claims, capability policies, compiler equivalence checks) before they can be accepted by tooling or registries.

../input/repository/learn/language-tour.qmd:26:## Evaluation is small on purpose
../input/repository/learn/language-tour.qmd:44:Vectors, maps, strings, bytes, symbols, integers, booleans, pairs, and `nil` are data. Updates return new persistent values. Closures, contracts, seals, native functions, and effect programs are opaque runtime values with stable hashing rules.
../input/repository/learn/language-tour.qmd:56:Type terms and inference are toolchain obligations, not extra evaluator special forms. This keeps TCB-A small while still allowing packages to require typechecking. Read [Types](../docs/spec/TYPES.md) for the accepted grammar and current inference coverage.
../input/repository/learn/effects-replay.qmd:21:The kernel evaluates this to a sealed request. It does not open a file.
../input/repository/learn/effects-replay.qmd:33:Anything absent from `allow` is denied. Per-operation policy narrows paths, remotes, programs, plugins, schemas, timeouts, and budgets. The exhaustive policy surface is in [`caps.toml`](../docs/spec/CAPS_TOML.md).
../input/repository/learn/effects-replay.qmd:35:## Run and record
../input/repository/learn/effects-replay.qmd:38:./target/debug/genesis run read_config.gc \
../input/repository/learn/effects-replay.qmd:44:Each entry records operation, payload hash, continuation hash, request hash, response hash, decision, capability descriptor, and scheduler facts where applicable.
../input/repository/learn/effects-replay.qmd:57:Capability denial, timeout, malformed bridge output, budget exhaustion, and unavailable backends become trusted sealed `ERROR` values at the boundary. Use `core/effect::catch` for recoverable branches; do not pattern-match a forged error-shaped map.
../input/repository/learn/effects-replay.qmd:60:The log is provenance, not permission. Replay validates what happened; `caps.toml` decides what may happen during a live run.
../input/repository/learn/agent-loop.qmd:3:description: "Bounded retrieval, exact intent selection, structured diagnostics, and evidence-backed repair."
../input/repository/learn/agent-loop.qmd:36:A safe repair must preserve policy and obligations, target the exact failing identity, rerun the original command, and abstain when preconditions changed. Never disable a gate to make its diagnostic disappear.

--- language tour ---
---
title: "Language tour"
description: "CoreForm syntax, immutable values, closures, module scope, contracts, and types."
---

# The language in one pass

## CoreForm is the interchange language

GenesisCode source is canonicalizable symbolic data. The reader supports:

```lisp
nil true false
42 -7
"utf-8 string"
b"raw bytes"
symbol :keyword
(a proper list)
["a" "vector"]
{:ordered "map" :answer 42}
'quoted-datum
```

Semicolon comments continue to line end. Strings and bytes support the escapes frozen in the [canonical syntax specification](../docs/spec/COREFORM_CANON_HASH.md).

## Evaluation is small on purpose

```lisp
(def sum-of-squares
  (fn (x y)
    (let ((xx (prim int/mul x x))
          (yy (prim int/mul y y)))
      (prim int/add xx yy))))

(if (prim int/lt 0 1)
  (sum-of-squares 3 4)
  0)
```

Source-level n-ary functions and calls desugar to unary currying. The kernel understands `quote`, `fn`, `if`, `begin`, `let`, `prim`, `seal`, `unseal`, top-level `def`, and application. Higher-level behavior belongs in libraries.

## Values are immutable

Vectors, maps, strings, bytes, symbols, integers, booleans, pairs, and `nil` are data. Updates return new persistent values. Closures, contracts, seals, native functions, and effect programs are opaque runtime values with stable hashing rules.

## Scope is lexical and modules are recursive

`def` is valid only at module top level. Top-level closures share a recursive module scope, so later definitions are visible to earlier closures and mutual recursion is possible. `let` creates lexical bindings and never mutates an outer scope.

## Contracts are behavior as data

Contracts provide message dispatch, delegation through a prototype, immutable extension, and explainability. `UNHANDLED` is a sealed protocol result: dispatch delegates only when trusted unsealing succeeds. User data that merely resembles it cannot redirect control flow.

## Types live outside the kernel

Type terms and inference are toolchain obligations, not extra evaluator special forms. This keeps TCB-A small while still allowing packages to require typechecking. Read [Types](../docs/spec/TYPES.md) for the accepted grammar and current inference coverage.

## Pure first, effects second

A pure function returns a value. A host-aware program returns an effect program assembled from `core/effect::pure`, `core/effect::perform`, `core/effect::bind`, and `core/effect::catch`. Continue with [effects and replay](effects-replay.qmd).

--- benchmark README ---
# GenesisCode Agent Task Benchmark v0.1

This public benchmark freezes 27 authoring tasks: nine task classes crossed with
small, medium, and large context packs. `suite.json` is the machine authority.

The reference candidates are public development oracles, not held-out material.
They prove that every workload is satisfiable through the production CLI. They
must not be used to claim held-out quality or model-independent aggregate scores.

Validate the suite with:

```sh
python3 scripts/lib/gc_task_benchmarks.py --check --self-test
cargo test -p gc_cli --test cli_agent_task_benchmarks
```
